subject: First Aid & Safety
title: Fire Prevention and Extinguishers
description: Learn Fire Prevention and Extinguishers in one minute.

hook.text: Using water on a grease fire doesn't extinguish it—it creates a fireball. Know your extinguisher.

buildup.text: Fire extinguishers are classified by the type of fire they fight: A, B, C, D, and K.

discovery.text: Class A for ordinary materials. Class B for liquids. Class C for electrical. Class K for kitchen.

twist.text: Most people have never used a fire extinguisher. In a real fire, unfamiliarity causes hesitation.

climax.text: PASS: Pull the pin, Aim at the base, Squeeze the handle, Sweep side to side.

punchline.text: Pull. Aim. Squeeze. Sweep. Four words that can stop a fire cold.

quiz.question: What does PASS stand for when using a fire extinguisher?
